---
title: "story_layout"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd) | [View source](https://codeberg.org/usrbinr/stwd/src/branch/main/R/stwd.R#L1460)

# `story_layout()`

Compose a Story Layout

## Usage

```r
story_layout(
  plot,
  title = NULL,
  subtitle = NULL,
  narrative = NULL,
  caption = NULL,
  narrative_position = "right",
  narrative_width = 0.35,
  title_size = 16,
  subtitle_size = 11,
  narrative_size = 10,
  caption_size = 9,
  title_height = NULL,
  subtitle_height = NULL,
  caption_height = NULL,
  auto_heights = TRUE,
  output_width = 12
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `plot` | The main ggplot visualization. |
| `title` | Optional title - either text (marquee-formatted) or a ggplot object created by `title_block()`. When text is provided, it's converted to a title_block with the specified title_size. |
| `subtitle` | Optional subtitle - either text (marquee-formatted) or a ggplot object created by `subtitle_block()`. |
| `narrative` | Optional narrative - either text (marquee-formatted) or a ggplot object created by `text_narrative()`. |
| `caption` | Optional caption - either text or a ggplot object created by `caption_block()`. |
| `narrative_position` | Position of narrative: "right", "left", "bottom". Default: "right". |
| `narrative_width` | Width proportion for narrative (0-1). Default: 0.35. |
| `title_size` | Size for title in pts. Default: 16 (large, prominent). |
| `subtitle_size` | Size for subtitle in pts. Default: 11. |
| `narrative_size` | Size for narrative text in pts. Default: 10. |
| `caption_size` | Size for caption in pts. Default: 9. |
| `title_height` | Height proportion for title row (0-1). When NULL and `auto_heights = TRUE`, this is calculated automatically based on text length. |
| `subtitle_height` | Height proportion for subtitle row (0-1). When NULL and `auto_heights = TRUE`, this is calculated automatically. |
| `caption_height` | Height proportion for caption row (0-1). When NULL and `auto_heights = TRUE`, this is calculated automatically. |
| `auto_heights` | Logical. If TRUE (default), automatically calculates appropriate heights for title/subtitle/caption based on text length to prevent clipping. Set to FALSE to use fixed heights. |
| `output_width` | Numeric. Expected output width in inches (default: 12). Used by auto_heights to estimate how many lines text will wrap to. |

## Value

A patchwork object.

## Description

Combines a chart with optional title, narrative text, and caption
into a complete story layout using patchwork. Each component is a
modular plot element, giving full control over sizing and positioning.

## Details

This function creates layouts inspired by
[Storytelling with Data](https://www.storytellingwithdata.com/) principles,
with large, prominent titles and supporting narrative text.

## Examples

```r
## Not run: 
library(ggplot2)
library(marquee)

p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = factor(cyl), y = mpg)) +
    geom_col(fill = "#404040") +
    theme_minimal()

# Basic usage with text
story_layout(
    plot = p,
    title = "**Fuel Efficiency by Cylinder Count**",
    subtitle = "{#E63946 4-cylinder} cars lead in MPG",
    narrative = "The data shows a clear trend:\n\n**Fewer cylinders = better mileage.**",
    caption = "Source: Motor Trend, 1974"
)

# Full control with block functions
story_layout(
    plot = p,
    title = title_block(
        "**Fuel Efficiency by Cylinder Count**",
        title_size = 20
    ),
    subtitle = subtitle_block(
        "{#E63946 4-cylinder} cars lead in MPG",
        subtitle_size = 12
    ),
    narrative = text_narrative(
        "The data shows a clear trend:\n\n**Fewer cylinders = better mileage.**",
        size = 11
    )
)

## End(Not run)
```
